exercises.name: Toe Yoga (Isolation Drill)
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 8-10 reps each pattern per foot
exercises.cue: Think of your toes as piano keys. Lift big toe while pressing four little toes down, then reverse. Move independently, not as a block.
exercises.notes: Entry point for all foot work. Builds neural vocabulary of the foot.

exercises.name: Short Foot Exercise (Foot Doming)
exercises.cue: Pull ball of foot toward heel without curling toes. Imagine picking up a marble with your arch, not your toes. Toes stay long and relaxed.
exercises.notes: Foundational arch control. Feeds into every weight shift during a dropback.

exercises.name: Tripod Foot Stance (Static Hold)
exercises.cue: Three points of a triangle -- heel, big toe knuckle, pinky toe knuckle -- equal pressure. Maintain domed arch.
exercises.notes: This IS the foot position during plant foot strike on every throw. Non-negotiable.

exercises.name: Towel Scrunches with Toe Spread
exercises.cue: Grip, pull, spread. Think of your foot like a hand -- open and close it with control.
exercises.notes: Builds gripping strength needed for traction in cleats on variable surfaces.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raise with Tripod Focus
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 10-12 reps each foot, 3s up / 3s down
exercises.cue: Rise straight up like an elevator, not a rollercoaster. If the ankle wobbles at the top, you went too high -- own the range you can control.
exercises.notes: Replicates push-off mechanics of back foot during a throw.

exercises.name: Banded Ankle 4-Way
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 10-15 reps each direction per foot
exercises.cue: Move from the ankle, not the knee. Keep the shin quiet. Control the return -- don't let the band snap you back.
exercises.notes: Builds multi-directional ankle resilience. QBs sustain ankle injuries from uneven pocket surfaces and off-platform throws.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Balance on Foam Pad (Eyes Closed)
exercises.cue: When you close your eyes, your foot becomes your eyes. Let it feel and react. Stay soft at the knee and ankle.
exercises.notes: Simulates proprioceptive demand of throwing while attention is directed upfield.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Hop to Stick (Multi-Direction)
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 5 reps each direction per foot
exercises.cue: Land like you're landing on thin ice -- quiet and controlled. Own the landing before you move on. If you can't stick it, shorten the distance.
exercises.notes: Directly mimics plant-foot strike when throwing on the move. Rotational variant mirrors rollout throw pivot.

exercises.name: Lateral Band Walk with Short Foot Engagement
exercises.cue: Band around forefoot. The band will try to collapse your arch inward on every step. Fight it. Keep the dome alive. Stay low in your hips.
exercises.notes: Lateral pocket movement with foot integrity -- shuffle and slide footwork QBs use constantly.

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL to Overhead Press (Barefoot)
exercises.cue: Your foot is the foundation of a skyscraper. If the base shifts, everything above crumbles. Press through the three points and keep the dome.
exercises.notes: Full kinetic chain demand of an off-platform throw -- single-leg base, hip hinge, overhead delivery.

exercises.name: Reactive Drop Landing to Throw (Single-Leg)
exercises.cue: Absorb, stabilize, deliver. Three things in under one second. If the throw is wild, your foot didn't get quiet fast enough.
exercises.notes: Ultimate expression of the progression. Replicates throwing off a reset, scramble, or off-platform situation.

exercises.name: Multi-Directional Agility Ladder with Perturbation
exercises.cue: The ladder is the pocket. The pushes are pass rushers. Your feet stay precise no matter what your upper body is dealing with.
exercises.notes: Highest-fidelity simulation of pocket movement under duress with cognitive load.

exercises.name: Crocodile Breathing (Prone)
exercises.cue: Belly pushes into the floor on inhale. Expand into the sides and back. Slow exhale.
exercises.notes: Entry point. Teaches belly breathing in the least compensatory position. QBs tend to be chest breathers from years of shoulder pads.

exercises.name: Supine 90/90 Hip Lift (Basic)
exercises.cue: Feet flat on wall, knees/hips at 90. Exhale fully to drop the ribs. Pause 3s at bottom of exhale. Inhale through nose without losing rib position.
exercises.notes: PRI staple. Restores diaphragm to domed position. Corrects extended posture QBs develop from looking downfield.

exercises.name: Supine Diaphragmatic Breathing with Hand Feedback
exercises.cue: One hand on chest, one on belly. Inhale 4 counts -- belly rises, chest stays still. Exhale 6-8 counts through pursed lips. The exhale is the money.
exercises.notes: Basic breathing pattern re-education. Foundation for all bracing work.

exercises.name: Dead Bug -- Arms Only (Breathing Focus)
exercises.cue: Low back glued to floor. Exhale and lock ribs down. Reach one arm overhead without back arching. If back lifts, you've lost it -- reset.
exercises.notes: First integration of breathing under movement demand.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Lift with Ball Squeeze and Reach
exercises.cue: Squeeze ball between knees, lift hips slightly, exhale fully and reach arms to ceiling. Breathe in without losing position. Feel hamstrings and inner thighs, not low back.
exercises.notes: Progresses the 90/90. Trains oblique slings and ZOA with shoulder flexion under rib control.

exercises.name: Quadruped Breathing (All-Fours Belly Breathing)
exercises.cue: Hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Let belly hang. Breathe into belly and sides -- a partner should see your flanks expand. No low back sag or rounding.
exercises.notes: 360-degree expansion against gravity. Bridges prone breathing to more demanding positions.

exercises.name: Dead Bug -- Contralateral Arms and Legs
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 8 reps each side, 3s out / 3s back
exercises.cue: Exhale, lock ribs down. Extend opposite arm and leg simultaneously. The moment your back arches, you're done with that set. Quality over quantity.
exercises.notes: Full dead bug. Cross-body coordination under anti-extension demand.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl Hold (Static)
exercises.cue: From all fours, tuck toes and lift knees 1 inch off ground. Hold. Breathe. If you're shaking, good -- breathe through it.
exercises.notes: Teaches maintaining a brace while breathing -- exactly what the pocket demands.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Pallof Press with Breath Hold
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 8 reps each side, full breath cycle per rep
exercises.cue: Inhale and brace 360. Press band out on exhale. Hold arms-extended for a full breath cycle. Do not let the band rotate you.
exercises.notes: Anti-rotation under breathing demand. Directly applicable to pocket poise.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl -- Slow Forward/Backward
exercises.cue: Opposite hand and foot move together. Knees 1 inch off ground the entire time. A cup of water on your back should not spill. Nasal breathing only.
exercises.notes: Dynamic core stability under locomotion. If you have to mouth-breathe, slow down.

exercises.name: Dead Bug with Band Resistance (Contralateral)
exercises.cue: Band looped around one foot and opposite hand. Exhale, lock it down, then extend. The band wants to pull you into extension -- do not let it.
exercises.notes: Where bracing becomes a skill, not just an exercise.

exercises.name: Turkish Get-Up -- Bottom Segment (Floor to Elbow)
exercises.cue: Breathe and brace before every transition. Each position is a checkpoint -- pause, breathe, own it, then move. If you rush, you missed the point.
exercises.notes: Multi-plane core stability under asymmetric loading.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl with Perturbation (Partner Push)
exercises.cue: Bear crawl while partner gives random pushes from side, front, or top. Stay low, keep breathing, do not collapse. This is pocket presence training for your core.
exercises.notes: Simulates chaotic force environment of a collapsing pocket.

exercises.name: Tall Kneeling Overhead Med Ball Catch and Throw
exercises.cue: Catch high, exhale hard on the throw. Your core is the link between the ground and the ball. If you flare, you leak power.
exercises.notes: Ballistic brace under reactive loading. Tests the breath-brace-throw sequence.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Stance Cable Chop with Breath Tempo
exercises.cue: Stand on one leg. Inhale at top of chop, exhale forcefully as you pull down and across. Every rep is a breath. If you lose balance, the core lost the argument.
exercises.notes: Throwing a football is a single-leg rotational power event. This trains the breath-brace-rotate sequence.

exercises.name: Supine Chin Tuck (Deep Neck Flexor Activation)
exercises.cue: Lying face up, draw chin straight back into the floor -- double chin. Feel a gentle contraction deep in the front of your neck. Keep jaw relaxed.
exercises.notes: Entry-level deep neck flexor activation. Daily exercise.

exercises.name: Deep Neck Flexor Endurance Hold
exercises.cue: Chin tuck, then lift head barely off the surface -- thickness of a sheet of paper. If front strap muscles bulge, you've gone too far. Reset lighter.
exercises.notes: Builds cervical endurance and motor control. Should be performed daily.

exercises.name: Seated Wall Slide (Scapular Upward Rotation)
exercises.cue: Back and head against wall, arms in goalpost position. Slide arms overhead -- wrists, elbows, and low back stay pinned to wall. The limitation is the point.
exercises.notes: Foundational scapular patterning. The range limitation reveals where mobility work is needed.

exercises.name: Prone Y-T-W Raises (Floor)
exercises.cue: Lie face down with chin tuck. Thumbs up for Y and T. For W, elbows bent at 90 pulled toward hips, externally rotate hands to ceiling. Hold 3-5s. Light and controlled.
exercises.notes: Y = lower trap, T = middle trap, W = external rotators and rhomboids. No shrugging.

exercises.name: Quadruped Chin Tuck with Neck Isometrics
exercises.cue: Pack your neck with a chin tuck. Partner applies gentle pressure in 4 directions. Resist and maintain position. Think steel rod from tailbone to crown.
exercises.notes: Deep neck flexors under anti-gravity load with cervical co-contraction.

exercises.name: Standing Wall Slide with Foam Roller
exercises.cue: Face wall, forearms on roller. Slide up while pressing into wall and protracting shoulder blades. Feel muscles along side of rib cage engage. Control descent 3s.
exercises.notes: Serratus anterior focus. Critical for scapular upward rotation during throwing.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Banded Neck Isometric with Pallof Hold
exercises.cue: Band around forehead pulling laterally + Pallof press from opposite direction. Resist both forces. Neck stays neutral, arms stay centered.
exercises.notes: Trains neck and core to stabilize against competing rotational forces -- exactly like the pocket.

exercises.name: Prone Y-T-W on Incline Bench with Light Dumbbells
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 10-12 reps each position, 2-3 lb DBs
exercises.cue: Chest-down on 30-45 degree bench with chin tuck. Depress shoulder blades -- pull them into back pockets -- THEN raise. If upper traps fire first, weight is too heavy.
exercises.notes: Loaded scapular stability. Key error: shrugging upper traps toward ears.

exercises.name: Tall Kneeling Banded Neck Flexion/Extension with Perturbations
exercises.cue: Band around head, partner applies quick unpredictable tugs. React and stabilize -- do not pre-brace. This trains reflexive stabilization for absorbing hits.
exercises.notes: Dynamic cervical stability against unpredictable forces. Requires no cervical instability or recent concussion history.

exercises.name: Overhead Carry with Packed Neck (Bottoms-Up KB)
exercises.cue: KB bottoms-up, locked out overhead. Pack neck with chin tuck. Walk slowly. Shoulder blade must be glued to ribcage -- if it wings or you lose neck position, weight is too heavy.
exercises.notes: Integrated overhead scapular + neck endurance under load.

exercises.name: Standing 4-Way Neck Machine (Eccentric Emphasis)
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 10-12 reps each direction, 2s concentric / 4-5s eccentric
exercises.cue: Concentric 2s, hold 1s, eccentric 4-5s. The slow eccentric is where neck injury resilience is built. Start conservatively -- neck responds to volume and consistency, not max load.
exercises.notes: Critical for concussion risk reduction. Must be supervised by strength staff. Build over weeks.

exercises.name: Drop Step to Throw with Perturbation
exercises.cue: 3-step drop, coach applies light push to torso/head as you set and throw. Maintain eye level, packed neck, clean scapular mechanics through the throw. If head dips or elbow drops, reduce intensity.
exercises.notes: Full kinetic chain integration. Late-stage return-to-sport or in-season performance drill.

exercises.name: Supine 90/90 Hip Breathing
exercises.cue: Feet flat on wall, knees/hips at 90. Inhale through nose to expand lower ribs laterally. Exhale fully, feel low back press gently into floor.
exercises.notes: Passive. Overlaps with bracing category. Entry point for hip and pelvic mobility.

exercises.name: Ankle Dorsiflexion Half-Kneeling Wall Stretch
exercises.cue: Half-kneeling, front foot 3-4 inches from wall. Drive knee over pinky toe without heel lifting. Hold 2s, return, repeat.
exercises.notes: Critical for drop-back mechanics and scrambling from the pocket. Often overlooked.

exercises.name: Open Book Thoracic Rotation (Side-lying)
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 8 reps each side, 3s hold at end range
exercises.cue: Knees stacked and pinned at 90 to lock out the lumbar spine. Reach top arm across and let gravity open the chest. Follow hand with eyes.
exercises.notes: Passive T-spine rotation. Essential for QBs -- throwing demands exceptional thoracic rotation.

exercises.name: Hip CARS (Standing)
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 3-5 reps each direction per hip
exercises.cue: Stand tall, brace everything except working hip. Lift knee up, rotate it out, extend behind you, then reverse. Circle as large as possible with no pelvis or spine compensation.
exercises.notes: Full-range hip joint articulation. Reveals where restrictions live.

exercises.name: Shoulder CARS (Standing)
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 3-5 reps each direction per arm
exercises.cue: Make a fist, tense the arm. Raise forward and overhead, rotate behind, bring down by your side. Biggest, slowest circle you can manage. If you lose control, that's your current edge.
exercises.notes: Full glenohumeral ROM assessment and maintenance. Essential daily practice for a throwing athlete.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Adductor Rockback
exercises.cue: One knee down, opposite leg extended straight to the side, foot flat. Sit hips back toward heel keeping chest tall. Deep inner-thigh stretch on extended leg.
exercises.notes: Adductor length directly impacts stride mechanics during throws.

exercises.name: Quadruped T-Spine CARS
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 5 reps each direction per side, 5s each direction
exercises.cue: Hand behind head, elbow down. Rotate elbow under body toward opposite knee, then reverse and open toward ceiling. Lumbar spine and hips should not move at all.
exercises.notes: Segmental thoracic control. Isolates T-spine rotation from lumbar compensation.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Switch (Shinbox)
exercises.cue: Both knees at 90. Windshield-wiper knees side to side. Get both knees to floor on each side, chest tall, feet planted. Hands off floor if possible.
exercises.notes: Hip IR and ER simultaneously. Minimum 25-30 degrees IR required. No medial knee pain.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Arm Bar with Rotation
exercises.cue: Light KB bottoms-up, press overhead from half-kneeling. Slowly rotate torso away from bell with arm locked out. Bell should not wobble.
exercises.notes: Shoulder stability through range combined with thoracic rotation under load.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch (with Rotation)
exercises.cue: Deep lunge, hands inside front foot. Drop back hip toward floor. Rotate inside arm to ceiling, follow with eyes. Hold 2 breaths, return, repeat.
exercises.notes: Multi-joint mobility complex. Hits hip flexors, adductors, T-spine, and ankles in one flow.

exercises.name: 90/90 Lift-Off (Hip IR/ER End-Range Strength)
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 5 reps each position per side, 5s hold
exercises.cue: In 90/90, lift back knee 1-2 inches using only hip IR -- no leaning, no hands. Then lift front knee using hip ER. These are humbling -- small movement is fine.
exercises.notes: Isometric end-range strength. Builds strength where you're most vulnerable. Key for QB stride mechanics.

exercises.name: Loaded Cossack Squat
exercises.cue: Goblet-hold KB. Shift laterally into deep side lunge, sit as low as possible over bent leg, straight leg stays extended with toes up. Chest tall throughout.
exercises.notes: Deep hip flexion, abduction, and adductor length under load.

exercises.name: Sleeper Stretch to Prone Shoulder CARS Combo
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 30s stretch + 3 CARS reps each side
exercises.cue: Side-lying, bottom arm at 90. Gently press hand toward floor for posterior capsule stretch (30s). Then prone -- full shoulder CARS hovering arm just off floor through entire arc.
exercises.notes: Passive then active combo. Targets posterior shoulder capsule -- often restricted in throwers.

exercises.name: Standing Bow (Hip Extension + Shoulder Flexion End-Range Hold)
exercises.cue: Single-leg, hinge and extend free leg behind while reaching arms overhead. Push back foot away as hard as you can and reach arms further -- maximal tension at both end ranges. You should be shaking.
exercises.notes: Simultaneous hip extension and shoulder flexion end-range control. Full-body tension.

exercises.name: Turkish Get-Up (Full, Slow Tempo)
exercises.cue: Moderate KB. Each position is a checkpoint -- pause 2s at every transition. Bell arm locked out and stacked over shoulder at all times. Eyes on bell until standing. One sloppy rep means weight is too heavy.
exercises.notes: Gold standard integration. Tests shoulder stability, hip transitions, T-spine extension in one continuous movement.

exercises.name: Standing Band External Rotation at 0 Degrees
exercises.cue: Elbow pinned to side at 90. Rotate forearm outward against band. Wrist neutral, squeeze shoulder blade back slightly. Control the return.
exercises.notes: Entry-level ER strengthening. Infraspinatus and teres minor. Precision exercise, not strength exercise.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Aparts
exercises.cue: Light band at shoulder height, arms straight. Pull apart by squeezing shoulder blades together until band touches chest. Keep ribs down -- no back arch.
exercises.notes: Rear delt and rhomboid activation. Simple but critical for scapular health.

exercises.name: Prone Y-T-W Raises
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 8-10 reps each letter, bodyweight or 2-3 lb DBs
exercises.cue: Face down. Y=arms at 120 degrees thumbs up. T=arms straight out thumbs up. W=elbows to hips, externally rotate hands up. Hold 2-3s each.
exercises.notes: Also appears in neck/shoulder category. Lower trap, middle trap, external rotators.

exercises.name: Sidelying External Rotation (Dumbbell)
exercises.cue: Lie on non-throwing side. Towel between elbow and rib cage. Elbow at 90. Rotate forearm toward ceiling. If you need momentum, weight is too heavy.
exercises.notes: Precision ER work. Isolates infraspinatus and teres minor better than standing variations.

exercises.name: Standing Band Internal Rotation
exercises.cue: Band anchored at elbow height. Elbow at 90 pinned to side. Rotate forearm inward across body. Control eccentric.
exercises.notes: Subscapularis strengthening. Throwing athletes are ER-dominant but subscapularis is the most commonly injured cuff muscle in throwers. Balance matters.

exercises.name: Wall Slides (Serratus Anterior Focus)
exercises.cue: Face wall, forearms flat. Slide arms up while pressing into wall and protracting shoulder blades. Feel serratus engage along rib cage side. Control descent.
exercises.notes: Serratus anterior is critical for scapular upward rotation during throwing.

exercises.name: 90/90 External Rotation (Thrower's Position)
exercises.prescription: 2-3 sets, 10-12 reps, band or light DB
exercises.cue: Arm at 90 abduction, elbow at 90 -- cocking phase position. Rotate forearm back and up, return with control. Shoulder blade pinched back.
exercises.notes: Most sport-specific rotator cuff position for QBs. Thrower's Ten staple.

exercises.name: Prone Horizontal Abduction at 90 (Full Can)
exercises.cue: Face down on bench, arm hanging. Raise arm to shoulder height with thumb to ceiling (full can). Squeeze shoulder blade at top.
exercises.notes: Core Thrower's Ten exercise. Full can position safer than empty can for supraspinatus tendon.

exercises.name: Band ER/IR at 90/90 with Rhythmic Stabilization
exercises.cue: 90/90 throwing position holding a ball. Partner applies quick unpredictable pushes. Resist and maintain. Trains reflexive stabilization that protects shoulder during throwing.
exercises.notes: Perturbation training. Ideally done with a partner or athletic trainer.

exercises.name: Turkish Get-Up (Partial or Full)
exercises.cue: Arm stays vertical and packed throughout. Start with no weight, then a shoe balanced on fist, then light KB. Shoulder stabilizes through multiple planes.
exercises.notes: Among the best integrated shoulder stability exercises. Also appears in mobility and bracing categories.

exercises.name: Jaeger Band Long-Toss Simulation Series
exercises.cue: Anchor J-Band behind you. Simulate full throwing motion against resistance -- stride, hip rotation, arm acceleration, follow-through. Emphasis on proper arm slot and smooth kinetic chain, NOT pulling hard.
exercises.notes: Capstone of the Jaeger arm care system. Mimics throwing motion under resistance. Must have healthy, pain-free shoulders.

exercises.name: Plyometric Ball Throws (90/90 and Overhead)
exercises.prescription: 2 sets, 15-20 reps with 1-2 lb plyo ball
exercises.cue: Rapid throw-catch-throw against rebounder or wall. Key is rapid eccentric loading that trains posterior cuff deceleration. NOT a heavy medicine ball.
exercises.notes: Trains deceleration forces of throwing (7000+ deg/sec IR). High-stress -- not daily. Never on heavy throwing days.

exercises.name: Quadriceps Setting (Quad Set)
exercises.cue: Press back of knee flat into floor, tighten top of thigh, hold 5s. Kneecap should glide slightly upward.
exercises.notes: Day-one exercise in most ACL rehab protocols. Safe and essential.

exercises.name: Prone Hip Extension (Glute Squeeze)
exercises.cue: Lying face down, squeeze one glute and lift leg 2-3 inches. Hip bones stay pressed into ground -- no low back arching.
exercises.notes: Basic glute activation. Foundational for all posterior chain work.

exercises.name: McGill Curl-Up
exercises.cue: One knee bent, one straight. Hands under low back arch. Lift only head and shoulders -- chest is a stiff board. No neck flexion.
exercises.notes: McGill Big 3 #1. Trains anterior core with minimal spinal load. Superior to sit-ups for low back pain.

exercises.name: Side-Lying Clamshell
exercises.cue: Heels together, knees stacked. Open top knee without rolling pelvis back. Feel it deep in side of hip, not TFL on the front.
exercises.notes: Weak glute medius correlates with knee valgus (ACL risk) and compensatory lumbar side-bending.

exercises.name: Bird-Dog (McGill Big 3)
exercises.cue: Hands and knees, extend opposite arm and leg without ANY pelvis or shoulder rotation. Imagine cup of water on low back. Don't let it sag.
exercises.notes: McGill Big 3 #2. Anti-rotation and anti-extension simultaneously.

exercises.name: Double-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.cue: Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top. Straight line from knees to shoulders. Do NOT hyperextend low back at top.
exercises.notes: Hamstring strength is critical for ACL protection -- they resist anterior tibial translation.

exercises.name: Side Plank (McGill Big 3)
exercises.cue: Elbow under shoulder, feet stacked or staggered. Lift hips, hold straight line. Squeeze bottom-side glute. Breathe normally. Start from knees if needed.
exercises.notes: McGill Big 3 #3. Lateral spine stability. Common weakness in QBs who rotate repeatedly to one side.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.cue: Extend one leg, drive through planted heel. Hips MUST stay level -- no dropping on free side. If pelvis rotates, you're not ready.
exercises.notes: Unilateral. Goal: less than 10% side-to-side asymmetry.

exercises.name: Lateral Band Walk (Monster Walk)
exercises.cue: Mini-band above ankles, quarter-squat position, chest up. Step laterally maintaining band tension. Toes forward, not turned out.
exercises.notes: Glute medius controls femoral IR and adduction -- the primary dynamic factor in knee valgus collapse (#1 non-contact ACL mechanism).

exercises.name: Pallof Press (Anti-Rotation)
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 10 reps each side, 3s hold extended
exercises.cue: Stand perpendicular to cable/band at chest height. Press handle out from sternum. Band tries to rotate you -- resist. No hip shift or trunk rotation.
exercises.notes: Essential anti-rotation pattern for QBs. Protects lumbar spine from excessive rotational shear during throwing.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
exercises.cue: Slight bend in stance knee. Hinge at hip, free leg reaches behind as counterbalance. Flat back -- broomstick from head to heel. DB in opposite hand.
exercises.notes: Excellent for ACL prevention once baseline strength established. Start bodyweight with wall fingertip touch.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.cue: Rear foot elevated on bench. Lower back knee toward floor. Front shin stays roughly vertical. Drive up through front heel. Trunk upright.
exercises.notes: Monitor for anterior knee pain, clicking, or instability. Start bodyweight, progress to goblet, then DBs. Avoid if knee effusion present.

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl (Eccentric)
exercises.cue: Kneel on pad, ankles anchored, arms crossed. Lower yourself toward ground as SLOWLY as possible. Catch with hands and push back up. If you can do more than 6 with good form, slow down.
exercises.notes: Strongest evidence base of any single exercise for hamstring injury prevention. FIFA 11+ ACL prevention staple. Not if active hamstring graft donor site.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Drop Landing to Stick
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 5 reps each leg from 6-inch box
exercises.cue: Step off box onto one leg. Land softly -- quiet feet. Knee tracks over second toe. No valgus collapse. Stick 3 full seconds.
exercises.notes: Essential return-to-sport test but must not be introduced prematurely. Valgus collapse on landing IS the ACL injury mechanism. Have a qualified person observe.

exercises.name: Rotational Medicine Ball Scoop Toss
exercises.cue: Athletic stance, med ball at back hip. Drive rotation from the hips -- NOT the low back. Lumbar stays stable while T-spine and hips generate rotation. Release into wall.
exercises.notes: QB low back pain often comes from generating rotation through lumbar spine instead of hips and T-spine. This trains the separation.

exercises.name: Double-Leg Pogo Hops (Ankle Bounces)
exercises.cue: Stay tall, stiff ankles, minimize ground contact time. Bounce like a pogo stick, not a squat.
exercises.notes: Minimal knee flexion demand makes this very safe. Watch for swelling post-session.

exercises.name: Box Step-Ups with Hold (6-8 inch box)
exercises.cue: Drive through heel of top foot, stand tall at top, hold 2s. Control the descent -- no dropping.
exercises.notes: Pre-plyometric. Builds eccentric control needed for landing. Keep box height low initially.

exercises.name: Squat Jump with Stick Landing
exercises.cue: Load the hips, explode up, land soft with knees tracking over toes -- freeze 3 seconds. Quiet feet.
exercises.notes: The stick on landing teaches deceleration control. Do NOT progress to continuous jumping until stick landings are consistently quiet. Watch for knee valgus.

exercises.name: Lateral Bound to Stick (Bilateral to Bilateral)
exercises.cue: Push off outside foot, land on both feet with soft knees, absorb and freeze. Start with small distances (12-18 inches).
exercises.notes: Lateral movements stress ACL differently than sagittal plane. Start very short. Knee valgus on landing is a red flag.

exercises.name: Box Jump with Step-Down (16-20 inch)
exercises.cue: Swing arms, jump UP not forward, land softly on top with full foot contact. Step down -- do NOT jump down.
exercises.notes: Jumping UP onto a box reduces landing forces vs flat ground. Always step down. Start conservative box height.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Hop to Stick Landing
exercises.cue: Small hop forward, land on same leg, absorb through hip and knee, freeze 3 seconds. Knee stays over second toe.
exercises.notes: Critical ACL rehab milestone. Start very small (6-12 inches). Compare landing quality between legs. Asymmetry means more time at this level.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Rotational Slam
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 8 reps each side, 6-10 lb ball
exercises.cue: Load the back hip, rotate explosively through the core, slam into ground in front of lead foot. This is your throwing power generator.
exercises.notes: Relatively knee-safe -- power comes from hips and core. QB-specific: trains rotational power chain used in throwing.

exercises.name: Drop Landing from Box (12-20 inch, progressive)
exercises.cue: Step off box -- do not jump off. Land on both feet simultaneously, absorb into quarter squat, freeze. Silent landing.
exercises.notes: Significantly higher ground reaction forces than box jumps. Deliberate stress test for reconstructed knee. ANY pain, apprehension, or asymmetry = regress.

exercises.name: Lateral Single-Leg Bound (Skater Hop) with Stick
exercises.prescription: 3 sets, 5 reps each direction, 2s stick between reps
exercises.cue: Push off laterally, land on opposite foot, sink and stabilize -- hold 2 seconds before next rep. Wide and controlled, not fast.
exercises.notes: Combines single-leg landing with lateral force vectors. Stick between reps is non-negotiable at this stage. Continuous skater hops are Level 5.

exercises.name: Depth Jump to Sprint (16-20 inch box)
exercises.cue: Step off box, spend as LITTLE time on the ground as possible, explode into 5-yard sprint. Think 'hot ground.' Minimize amortization phase.
exercises.notes: Highest-impact drill. Ground reaction forces 4-7x bodyweight. Full return-to-sport clearance required. Never perform fatigued. Very low volume.

exercises.name: Reactive Lateral Shuffle to Sprint
exercises.cue: Shuffle laterally in athletic stance. On visual cue, plant and explode into 5-yard sprint. Stay low in hips during shuffle.
exercises.notes: Unplanned cutting/COD is the primary ACL injury mechanism. Start with PLANNED direction changes, then progress to reactive. QB pocket movement and scramble patterns depend on this.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Box Jump (12-16 inch)
exercises.cue: Drive free knee up, explode off one leg, land on BOTH feet on top of box. Always land bilaterally on top -- single-leg component is takeoff only. Step down.
exercises.notes: Elite-level. Landing should always be bilateral to reduce single-leg impact. Only after demonstrated symmetry in all lower-level single-leg tests.

exercises.name: Walking Knee Hugs
exercises.cue: Pull knee to chest, stand tall on plant leg, squeeze glute of stance leg. Walk forward with each rep.
exercises.notes: General warm-up. Hip flexor stretch with glute activation.

exercises.name: Leg Swings (Front-to-Back and Lateral)
exercises.cue: Keep swinging leg straight, brace core, let ROM increase gradually. No forcing end range.
exercises.notes: Dynamic flexibility. Good for hip mobility warm-up before any session.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge (Two-Leg)
exercises.cue: Drive through heels, squeeze glutes hard at top for 2 seconds. Don't hyperextend low back.
exercises.notes: Glute activation. Also appears in rehab/prehab category.

exercises.name: Band Walk (Lateral Mini-Band Shuffle)
exercises.cue: Quarter-squat, keep band tension at all times, push knees out. Don't let them cave. 10 steps each direction.
exercises.notes: Glute medius activation before any lower body or throwing work.

exercises.name: Inchworm to World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.cue: Walk hands out to push-up position, step one foot outside the hand, rotate same-side arm to sky, hold 2s, return and walk hands back.
exercises.notes: Full-body integration warm-up. Hits hamstrings, T-spine, hip flexors, shoulders in one flow.

exercises.name: Lateral Lunge to Overhead Reach
exercises.cue: Push hips back as you step laterally, trailing leg stays straight. Reach both arms overhead at bottom to open T-spine.
exercises.notes: Adductor mobility + thoracic extension in one movement.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.cue: Extend one leg straight, drive through heel of working leg. Hips stay level -- no rotation or sag on unsupported side.
exercises.notes: Unilateral glute activation. Good bridge between activation and dynamic work.

exercises.name: A-Skip / High Knee Skip
exercises.cue: Drive knee up aggressively, strike ground with stiff ankle. Opposite arm drives forward. Torso tall and rhythmic.
exercises.notes: CNS priming. Transitions from activation to dynamic movement prep.

exercises.name: Lateral Bound to Stick (Single-Leg Landing)
exercises.cue: Push off laterally, land softly on outside leg, freeze 2 seconds. Knee tracks over toe, no wobble.
exercises.notes: Dynamic warm-up progression. Also appears in plyos category.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Rotational Scoop Toss
exercises.cue: Load back hip, rotate explosively from ground up, release ball. Arms follow hips -- don't muscle it with shoulders.
exercises.notes: CNS activation for rotational power. Mimics throwing kinetic chain.

exercises.name: Reactive Drop Step to Sprint (3-Step Drop Simulation)
exercises.cue: Start under center. On cue, 3-step drop at max speed, plant, drive forward into 10-yard sprint. Focus on explosive hip snap at plant.
exercises.notes: QB-specific CNS activation. Late-stage warm-up only after full general warm-up.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Rotational Med Ball Slam
exercises.cue: Balance on drive leg, load rotation away from target, rotate and slam. Stay balanced on one leg throughout -- if falling off, regress to two feet.
exercises.notes: Highest CNS demand warm-up exercise. Only when all lower levels are automatic.
